title: Canonical: response_format / JSON schema / structured outputs
body:
## Goal
Add a dialect-neutral representation of structured-output / JSON-mode configuration to `canonical.Request` so translation paths can carry response format constraints without silent loss.

## Context
Today `canonical.Request` only models sampling + tools (`Model`, `MaxTokens`, `Temperature`, `TopP`, `StopSequences`, `Tools`, `ToolChoice`). OpenAI `response_format` (including `json_object` and `json_schema` / structured outputs) is dropped on ingress parse. Anthropic and Google have equivalent controls (`output_config.format`, `responseMimeType` + `responseSchema`) that cannot round-trip through canonical.

Passthrough (same-family) retains full fidelity; this issue is for the **translated** path only.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `canonical.Request` gains a structured field (e.g. `ResponseFormat *ResponseFormat`) covering at least:
  - mode / kind: `text` | `json_object` | `json_schema` (names may be internal)
  - optional JSON Schema document (`json.RawMessage` or typed wrapper)
  - optional schema name / description when present on wire
  - optional strictness flag when present on wire
- [ ] Zero-value / nil means “no structured output requested” (current behavior).
- [ ] Unit tests assert marshal/unmarshal stability of the canonical field (no provider-specific field names in the type comments as required wire names).
- [ ] README translation table notes structured outputs as supported once mapping issues land (or “canonical ready; mapping TBD” if only this issue lands).
- [ ] No change to passthrough byte path.

## Implementation notes
- Prefer a small closed enum + optional schema blob over an `Extra map` so drop lists stay explicit.
- Do not invent provider defaults when unset.
- Keep schema as raw JSON to avoid re-implementing JSON Schema validation in the gateway.
- Related mapping work is separate issues (OpenAI ↔ Anthropic ↔ Google).

## Out of scope
- Provider capability gating / rejecting unsupported formats per model
- OpenAI Responses API exclusive shapes beyond chat-completions `response_format`
- Validating that client schema is well-formed JSON Schema

## Related
- Follow-up: Translate `response_format` OpenAI ↔ Anthropic `output_config.format` ↔ Google `responseMimeType`+`responseSchema`
- `canonical/canonical.go` `Request`
- README “Translation (cross family)” feature table

title: Translate response_format OpenAI ↔ Anthropic output_config.format ↔ Google responseMimeType+responseSchema
body:
## Goal
Map structured-output configuration bidirectionally across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google on the translation path using the canonical `ResponseFormat` model.

## Context
Once canonical carries response format, each dialect’s ingress parse and egress build must map:
- **OpenAI:** `response_format: {type: json_object|json_schema, json_schema?: {...}}`
- **Anthropic:** `output_config.format` (JSON schema structured outputs)
- **Google:** `generationConfig.responseMimeType` (`application/json`) + `responseSchema` / `responseJsonSchema`

Today these fields are absent from wire structs and silently dropped.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] OpenAI ingress parses `response_format` into canonical; OpenAI egress emits equivalent wire.
- [ ] Anthropic ingress/egress maps to/from `output_config.format` (or current Anthropic Messages structured-output field name as implemented upstream).
- [ ] Google ingress/egress maps to/from `responseMimeType` + schema fields on `generation_config`.
- [ ] Matrix tests (unit, not live network) for each direction:
  - OpenAI → Anthropic, OpenAI → Google
  - Anthropic → OpenAI, Anthropic → Google
  - Google → OpenAI, Google → Anthropic
- [ ] `json_object` without schema: best-effort map (e.g. Google `application/json` without schema; Anthropic documented fallback or explicit drop with test).
- [ ] `json_schema` with schema: schema body preserved byte-identical where providers accept JSON Schema subset; document any required transforms.
- [ ] When target dialect has no equivalent, policy is **documented + tested**: either drop with metric/comment in golden drop list, or return `bad_request` — choose one and apply consistently.
- [ ] Streaming + non-streaming both carry the request-side config (response body shape still text/JSON content as today).

## Implementation notes
- Extend `ingress/*/wire.go` + `parse.go` and `egress/*/wire.go` + `build.go`.
- Prefer not rewriting schema keywords unless a provider hard-requires it; if rewrite is required, unit-test the transform.
- Keep passthrough paths untouched.

## Out of scope
- Constrained decoding correctness guarantees from upstream models
- Tool/function JSON schema vs response schema confusion (tools remain separate)
- Prompt injection of “respond in JSON” as a substitute for native format APIs

## Related
- Depends on: Canonical response_format / JSON schema / structured outputs
- Golden fixtures suite for translation drops

title: Canonical: reasoning / thinking config (effort, budget, adaptive, display)
body:
## Goal
Represent request-side reasoning/thinking **configuration** in canonical form so effort, token budget, adaptive modes, and display preferences survive cross-dialect translation.

## Context
Canonical already has `BlockThinking` for **content** (thinking text + signature) and stream `EventThinkingDelta`. It does **not** model request knobs:
- OpenAI: `reasoning_effort` (and related reasoning controls on newer models)
- Anthropic: `thinking` / budget tokens / adaptive thinking flags
- Google: `thinkingConfig` (budget, include thoughts, etc.)

Without a config object, translate paths cannot enable thinking on the egress provider even when the client requested it.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `canonical.Request` gains something like `Thinking *ThinkingConfig` with fields sufficient for the three dialects, e.g.:
  - enabled / type (enabled, disabled, adaptive — as needed)
  - effort level (string or enum: minimal/low/medium/high/…)
  - budget tokens (`*int`)
  - include / display thoughts to client (`*bool` or display mode)
- [ ] Nil config means “client did not request thinking controls” (do not invent budgets).
- [ ] Document semantic mapping notes in code comments: effort ↔ budget is best-effort, not bit-identical.
- [ ] Unit tests for zero-value vs explicit disable vs explicit budget.
- [ ] No change to existing `BlockThinking` content model in this issue (content round-trip is separate).

## Implementation notes
- Keep provider-specific raw extras out of the hot path unless a field is truly unrepresentable; prefer first-class fields for the common set.
- Do not enable thinking by default for any provider.

## Out of scope
- Wire mapping (separate issue)
- Billing of reasoning tokens (usage details issue)
- Model capability matrix (“this model ignores thinking”)

## Related
- OpenAI `reasoning_effort` ↔ Anthropic thinking ↔ Google `thinkingConfig`
- Round-trip `reasoning_content` on assistant history
- Streaming: preserve reasoning deltas across dialects

title: OpenAI reasoning_effort ↔ Anthropic thinking ↔ Google thinkingConfig
body:
## Goal
Translate request-side reasoning/thinking controls across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google using canonical `ThinkingConfig`.

## Context
Clients often enable reasoning on one dialect and route to another provider. Today thinking **content** can appear in responses when the upstream emits it, but request knobs are not parsed/built, so cross-family calls never turn on thinking.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] OpenAI ingress: parse `reasoning_effort` (and any co-located reasoning object fields the wire already uses) → canonical.
- [ ] OpenAI egress: emit `reasoning_effort` / reasoning object from canonical when set.
- [ ] Anthropic ingress/egress: map `thinking` / budget_tokens (and adaptive fields if present on Messages API) ↔ canonical.
- [ ] Google ingress/egress: map `generationConfig.thinkingConfig` (budget, includeThoughts, etc.) ↔ canonical.
- [ ] Cross-dialect matrix unit tests for enable + budget/effort combinations.
- [ ] Documented best-effort table, e.g. effort `low|medium|high` ↔ approximate token budgets when only one side has numeric budget.
- [ ] Explicit disable maps to provider “off” when available; otherwise omit field (tested).
- [ ] Include-thoughts / display preference affects whether egress asks for visible reasoning; response-side content still uses existing thinking blocks.

## Implementation notes
- Extend wire structs carefully; avoid breaking unknown-field ignore behavior on passthrough (passthrough does not use these builders).
- Prefer omitting fields when unset rather than sending zeros that might enable thinking accidentally.

## Out of scope
- Guaranteeing all models honor the control
- Mapping proprietary vendor extensions outside OpenAI/Anthropic/Google
- Multi-turn history content (covered by reasoning_content round-trip issue)

## Related
- Depends on: Canonical reasoning / thinking config
- Round-trip reasoning_content on assistant history
- Usage details: reasoning_tokens

title: Round-trip reasoning_content on assistant history (OpenAI egress must not drop thinking)
body:
## Goal
Preserve assistant thinking/reasoning on multi-turn history when translating, especially OpenAI egress which currently drops `BlockThinking`.

## Context
- OpenAI **response** serialize maps `BlockThinking` → `reasoning_content` (`ingress/openai/serialize.go`).
- OpenAI **stream** emits thinking as `reasoning_content` deltas.
- OpenAI **request** parse does **not** read `reasoning_content` on assistant messages (`chatMessage` has no field; `parseMessages` never creates `BlockThinking`).
- OpenAI **egress** `buildAssistant` only handles text + tool_use — **thinking blocks are silently dropped** when building upstream OpenAI-compatible requests.
- Anthropic/Google already build/parse thinking blocks more completely.

This breaks tool-loops and multi-turn agents that need prior reasoning carried forward (DeepSeek/Kimi/Z.AI style `reasoning_content`, Claude thinking signatures, Gemini `thought` parts).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] OpenAI ingress: assistant `reasoning_content` (string or JSON string form already emitted) parses into `BlockThinking`.
- [ ] OpenAI egress: `BlockThinking` serializes to `reasoning_content` on assistant messages (not dropped).
- [ ] Anthropic egress continues to emit `thinking` + `signature` when present; OpenAI→Anthropic history keeps thinking blocks.
- [ ] Google egress continues to emit `thought: true` parts from thinking blocks; history round-trips.
- [ ] Multi-turn fixture: assistant message with text + reasoning + tool_calls → translate to each provider → thinking not empty on wire.
- [ ] Signature: when present (Anthropic), preserved through canonical; when OpenAI has no signature field, signature may be empty without dropping text.
- [ ] Regression test specifically named for “OpenAI egress must not drop thinking”.

## Implementation notes
- Align wire field with existing response path (`reasoning_content`).
- If multiple thinking blocks exist, define concatenation vs multi-field policy and test it.
- Coordinate with redacted_thinking support so redacted blocks are not coerced into plain reasoning_content incorrectly.

## Out of scope
- Request-side `reasoning_effort` config (separate)
- Provider rejection of history thinking (capability/docs only)
- Changing passthrough behavior

## Related
- DeepSeek/Kimi/Z.AI reasoning_content multi-turn tool-loop middleware
- redacted_thinking blocks support
- Streaming: preserve reasoning deltas across dialects

title: Canonical BlockDocument / PDF content blocks (Anthropic document, Google file/inline PDF)
body:
## Goal
Add a canonical content block for non-image documents (primarily PDF) so Anthropic `document` blocks and Google inline/file PDF parts can translate without collapsing to text placeholders.

## Context
Canonical blocks today: `text`, `image`, `tool_use`, `tool_result`, `thinking`. Anthropic Messages supports `document` sources (base64 PDF / URL). Google accepts PDF via `inline_data` / `file_data`. OpenAI chat multimodal is image-centric; document support may be file parts or degraded policy.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New block type e.g. `BlockDocument` (or equivalent name) with source kind `base64` | `url` | `file_id`/`file_uri` as needed.
- [ ] Fields: media type (e.g. `application/pdf`), data/uri, optional title/filename, optional metadata passthrough.
- [ ] Anthropic ingress/egress: `document` blocks map to/from canonical.
- [ ] Google ingress/egress: PDF `inline_data` / `file_data` map to/from canonical when mime is document.
- [ ] OpenAI policy documented: support if wire has a clear part type; otherwise explicit drop or error (tested).
- [ ] Unit tests for base64 PDF round-trip Anthropic ↔ Google at minimum.
- [ ] Does not break existing image block handling (images stay `BlockImage`).

## Implementation notes
- Reuse patterns from `ImageSource` where possible; avoid overloading `BlockImage` for PDFs.
- Size limits remain the proxy body limit; no new fetch service required in this issue unless image-URL fetch is already shared.

## Out of scope
- OCR / extracting PDF text as a gateway feature
- Arbitrary office formats beyond what providers accept
- Media generation APIs

## Related
- Google file_data / file_uri parts in translate
- OpenAI input_audio + file content parts parse/serialize
- Multimodal design spec chat extensions

title: Canonical BlockAudio for chat input
body:
## Goal
Introduce `BlockAudio` for **chat input** audio parts in the canonical model so dialects that accept audio in messages can translate.

## Context
Design spec (`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-18-multimodal-gateway-design.md`) already sketches:
- `Kind`: `base64` | `url`
- `MediaType`, `Data`
- Optional `Transcript` if client-supplied

Chat path currently has no audio block; STT/TTS are separate modalities.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `canonical.BlockType` includes `BlockAudio` (or agreed name) and `Block` fields for audio source + optional transcript.
- [ ] Document that this is **input-only** for chat (not TTS output as a content block).
- [ ] Unit tests construct requests with audio blocks without panics in existing builders (unknown block skip vs implement — builders updated in companion issues).
- [ ] README / design cross-link updated when implemented.

## Implementation notes
- Keep orthogonal to `/v1/audio/*` routes.
- Prefer same Kind/MediaType/Data pattern as images for consistency.

## Out of scope
- Full OpenAI/Google wire mapping (companion issues)
- Realtime / Live WebSocket audio
- Transcoding audio codecs in the gateway

## Related
- OpenAI input_audio + file content parts parse/serialize
- Google file_data / file_uri parts in translate
- Multimodal implementation plan PR4

title: OpenAI input_audio + file content parts parse/serialize
body:
## Goal
Parse and serialize OpenAI chat content parts for `input_audio` and file-like parts into/from canonical blocks.

## Context
`ingress/openai/parse.go` `parseContentBlocks` only handles `text` and `image_url`. `contentPart` has no audio/file fields. Unknown part types are ignored. Egress user builder only emits text + image_url.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Ingress accepts `input_audio` parts (format + base64 data per OpenAI chat shape) → `BlockAudio`.
- [ ] Ingress accepts file content parts if present in chat-completions multimodal schema used by clients (map to `BlockDocument` or agreed block); if unsupported, explicit tested drop/error policy.
- [ ] Egress OpenAI rebuilds `input_audio` / file parts from canonical when targeting OpenAI-compatible upstreams.
- [ ] Round-trip unit tests: OpenAI request JSON → canonical → OpenAI wire preserves audio payload and media type.
- [ ] Mixed content arrays (text + image + audio) preserve order as well as possible.
- [ ] Invalid audio part (missing data) returns validation error with OpenAI error envelope path unchanged.

## Implementation notes
- Extend `contentPart` and image/audio helpers; keep string content path working.
- Coordinate field names with current OpenAI chat-completions docs used by the project.

## Out of scope
- Fetching remote audio URLs server-side (unless shared media fetch already exists)
- Whisper transcription as side effect of chat
- Realtime API

## Related
- Depends on: Canonical BlockAudio; Canonical BlockDocument as needed
- Fix Google egress image URL (fetch/pass-through patterns may be reusable)

title: Google file_data / file_uri parts in translate
body:
## Goal
Support Google Gemini `file_data` / file URI parts on both ingress and egress translation paths for images, PDFs, and other accepted mime types.

## Context
Google egress `part` currently models `inline_data`, text, function call/response, and `thought`. Remote images are degraded to text placeholders (`[image: url]`). File API references (`file_data.file_uri`) are not modeled, so clients using Files API cannot cross-translate cleanly.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Wire `part` includes `file_data` (`mime_type` + `file_uri`) on ingress and egress.
- [ ] Canonical mapping: file URI sources on `BlockImage` / `BlockDocument` / `BlockAudio` as appropriate by mime type (document policy from BlockDocument issue).
- [ ] Google egress: when canonical has URL/file reference that Gemini accepts as `file_data`, emit `file_data` instead of text placeholder.
- [ ] Google ingress: parse `file_data` into canonical without dropping.
- [ ] Unit tests for image and PDF mime types.
- [ ] Document interaction with inline base64 path (prefer preserving original kind when possible).

## Implementation notes
- Do not require the gateway to upload to Google File API in this issue; pass through URIs the client already obtained when possible.
- Align with image URL fix issue for http(s) URLs without file API.

## Out of scope
- Implementing a gateway-side File API upload proxy
- GCS auth beyond forwarding client-provided URIs

## Related
- Fix Google egress image URL (stop text placeholder; support fetch or pass-through)
- Canonical BlockDocument / PDF content blocks
- Canonical BlockAudio

title: Fix Google egress image URL (stop text placeholder; support fetch or pass-through)
body:
## Goal
Stop degrading remote image URLs to text placeholders when building native Gemini requests; implement a correct pass-through or fetch-to-inline strategy.

## Context
In `egress/google/build.go`, non-base64 images become:
```go
c.Parts = append(c.Parts, part{Text: "[image: " + b.Image.Data + "]"})
```
This silently destroys vision fidelity for OpenAI/Anthropic clients that send `image_url` / URL sources to a `google` provider.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] No `[image: …]` text placeholder remains as the default behavior for http(s) or data URLs.
- [ ] Policy implemented and documented, choose one primary path (or ordered fallback):
  1. **Pass-through** via `file_data` / provider-supported URL mechanism when available, or
  2. **Fetch** URL server-side → `inline_data` with detected mime type (respect size limits, timeouts, SSRF controls).
- [ ] data: URLs already representable as base64 are normalized to `inline_data` without network I/O.
- [ ] Failure mode is explicit: request error or structured skip with hook/log — not silent text substitution (if skip allowed, golden drop list must list it).
- [ ] Unit tests with httptest fake image server for fetch path; unit tests for base64 path unchanged.
- [ ] SSRF protections if fetch is implemented (block link-local/metadata IPs per SECURITY.md norms).
- [ ] Proxy integration or build test: OpenAI image_url → Google body contains `inline_data` or `file_data`, not placeholder text.

## Implementation notes
- Reuse any shared media helpers under `proxy/media.go` if applicable.
- Keep Anthropic URL image sources working as today on Anthropic egress.

## Out of scope
- Hosting a public image CDN
- Video URL fetch
- Changing passthrough Google→Google behavior

## Related
- Google file_data / file_uri parts in translate
- SECURITY.md for outbound fetch policy

title: Canonical top_k + map Anthropic/Google
body:
## Goal
Carry `top_k` through canonical and map it on Anthropic and Google translation paths.

## Context
Canonical `Request` has `Temperature` and `TopP` but not `TopK`. Anthropic and Gemini support `top_k`; OpenAI chat-completions generally does not. Field is dropped today.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `canonical.Request` adds `TopK *int` (or equivalent).
- [ ] Anthropic ingress/egress parse/build `top_k`.
- [ ] Google ingress/egress map to `generationConfig.topK` / `top_k`.
- [ ] OpenAI ingress: ignore absent field; if a client sends vendor extension `top_k`, either parse into canonical or document drop (tested).
- [ ] OpenAI egress: omit `top_k` (not valid on standard OpenAI) without error.
- [ ] Unit tests for Anthropic ↔ Google preservation of top_k.

## Implementation notes
- Pointer distinguishes unset vs 0 if 0 is meaningful; follow temperature/top_p pattern.

## Out of scope
- Sampling math guarantees
- Provider max bounds validation beyond obvious non-negative checks

## Related
- frequency_penalty / presence_penalty through canonical
- seed through canonical

title: frequency_penalty / presence_penalty through canonical
body:
## Goal
Preserve OpenAI-style `frequency_penalty` and `presence_penalty` through canonical translation where providers support them.

## Context
These fields are not on canonical `Request` and are dropped on OpenAI ingress parse. Some Google/OpenAI-compatible providers accept them; Anthropic Messages generally does not.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Canonical fields `FrequencyPenalty *float64` and `PresencePenalty *float64`.
- [ ] OpenAI ingress/egress round-trip both fields.
- [ ] Google mapping if native or openai-compat egress supports them; else documented drop on pure native Google with golden test.
- [ ] Anthropic egress: omit (no error) when set; document in drop list.
- [ ] Unit tests for OpenAI → OpenAI-compat translate path retaining penalties.

## Implementation notes
- Do not clamp silently unless provider requires; if clamping, test bounds.

## Out of scope
- logit_bias, logprobs, n (except separate multi-choice policy issue)

## Related
- seed through canonical
- Golden fixtures suite for translation drops

title: seed through canonical
body:
## Goal
Thread `seed` through canonical for deterministic sampling where providers support it.

## Context
README lists OpenAI `seed` among **dropped** translation fields. Canonical has no seed field. Google `generationConfig.seed` exists on some APIs; Anthropic support is limited/absent.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `canonical.Request` includes `Seed *int64` (or `*int`).
- [ ] OpenAI ingress/egress maps `seed`.
- [ ] Google ingress/egress maps seed when present on wire.
- [ ] Anthropic: documented drop or map if API gains support; tests lock the policy.
- [ ] README translation table updates `seed` from dropped → yes (with caveats).
- [ ] Unit tests prove OpenAI → Google seed appears in `generation_config`.

## Implementation notes
- Seed does not guarantee bit-identical outputs across providers; document that fidelity is field-level, not reproducibility-level.

## Out of scope
- system_fingerprint response semantics (separate optional passthrough issue)
- Forcing temperature=0 when seed set

## Related
- System fingerprint / service_tier optional passthrough
- Golden fixtures suite for translation drops

title: parallel_tool_calls / disable_parallel_tool_use mapping
body:
## Goal
Map parallel tool-call controls between OpenAI and Anthropic (and Google if applicable) via canonical.

## Context
- OpenAI: `parallel_tool_calls` boolean on chat-completions.
- Anthropic: `tool_choice` may include `disable_parallel_tool_use` (or equivalent thinking/tool config flags depending on API version).
- Neither is modeled in canonical today; tool choice only has mode + name.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Canonical represents parallel tool-call preference (e.g. `ParallelToolCalls *bool` on Request or nested under ToolChoice).
- [ ] OpenAI ingress/egress maps `parallel_tool_calls`.
- [ ] Anthropic ingress/egress maps `disable_parallel_tool_use` with correct boolean polarity (`parallel=false` ↔ `disable_parallel_tool_use=true`).
- [ ] Google: map if API supports an equivalent; otherwise explicit drop test.
- [ ] Unit tests cover true/false/unset for OpenAI ↔ Anthropic both directions.
- [ ] Unset means omit field (provider default), not force false.

## Implementation notes
- Polarity bugs are easy — name tests after the wire fields.
- Do not change tool_choice mode mapping (`required` ↔ `any`) behavior.

## Out of scope
- Enforcing single tool call by post-processing responses
- Tool result ordering changes

## Related
- Non-function OpenAI tools policy
- Tool result multimodal content

title: Anthropic cache_control passthrough on translate (or document PT-only)
body:
## Goal
Decide and implement a clear policy for Anthropic `cache_control` breakpoints under translation: either carry them on Anthropic egress or document that they are **passthrough-only**.

## Context
Prompt caching is high-value for Claude Code and long system prompts. Same-dialect Anthropic→Anthropic passthrough already preserves bytes. Cross-dialect translate rebuilds messages from canonical and will strip `cache_control` on system/tools/blocks unless modeled.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Written policy in issue resolution / README caveats:
  - **Option A (implement):** canonical optional cache hint on system blocks / tools / content blocks; Anthropic egress re-emits `cache_control`.
  - **Option B (document):** translation drops `cache_control`; only passthrough preserves caching — document + golden drop test.
- [ ] If Option A: unit tests for system block and tool-level cache_control round-trip on Anthropic egress; OpenAI/Google ingress ignore unknown cache fields safely.
- [ ] If Option B: golden fixture asserts drop; README states PT-only for prompt caching.
- [ ] Either option must not break passthrough caching.

## Implementation notes
- Prefer Option B only if Option A is too lossy across dialects; product default should favor implementing Anthropic→Anthropic via passthrough and documenting cross-family limits.
- Cache TTL / ephemeral types should match Anthropic wire enums if implemented.

## Out of scope
- Non-Anthropic provider prompt caching APIs
- Automatic insertion of cache breakpoints by the gateway

## Related
- Anthropic cache usage fields on translated responses
- Usage details: cached_tokens

title: Usage details: cached_tokens, reasoning_tokens in hooks and OpenAI usage
body:
## Goal
Expose fine-grained token details (`cached_tokens`, `reasoning_tokens` / completion token details) on canonical usage, OpenAI serialized usage, and `hooks.UsageEvent`.

## Context
- `canonical.Usage` only has `InputTokens`, `OutputTokens`, `HasUsage`.
- OpenAI `usage` struct only has prompt/completion/total.
- `hooks.UsageEvent` only has `tokens_in` / `tokens_out`.
- Providers report cache hits and reasoning tokens that billing systems need.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Extend `canonical.Usage` with optional detail fields, e.g. `CacheReadTokens`, `CacheWriteTokens`, `ReasoningTokens` (names agreed in implementation).
- [ ] OpenAI response + stream usage serialization includes `prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens` and `completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens` when known (match OpenAI shape).
- [ ] Egress parsers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) populate details when upstream provides them.
- [ ] `hooks.UsageEvent` gains additive fields (omitempty) for cache and reasoning tokens without breaking existing consumers.
- [ ] JSONL and webhook sinks emit the new fields when non-zero.
- [ ] Unit tests for parse → serialize → hook event pipeline.
- [ ] Still **exactly one** usage event per chat request.

## Implementation notes
- Keep total `tokens_in`/`tokens_out` semantics stable (do not double-count reasoning into output unless upstream already does).
- Document whether reasoning tokens are included in `tokens_out` per provider.

## Out of scope
- Price calculation
- Media usage fields (already separate)

## Related
- Anthropic cache usage fields on translated responses
- Canonical reasoning / thinking config
- README Event schema section

title: Anthropic cache usage fields on translated responses
body:
## Goal
When Anthropic returns cache creation/read token counts, surface them on translated OpenAI/Google responses and hooks.

## Context
Anthropic usage includes fields like `cache_read_input_tokens` and `cache_creation_input_tokens`. Current `outUsage` / egress parse only maps `input_tokens` and `output_tokens`. Translation to OpenAI therefore loses cache accounting.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Anthropic egress (and ingress response parse if needed) reads cache usage fields into canonical usage details.
- [ ] OpenAI serialize maps into `prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens` (and documents creation tokens if no OpenAI analogue — e.g. hook-only or extension field).
- [ ] Anthropic client dialect serialize preserves Anthropic cache fields on Anthropic responses when translating from another provider only if those details exist (usually N/A).
- [ ] Streaming final usage events include cache details when provided on `message_delta` usage.
- [ ] Unit tests with fixture JSON from Anthropic including cache fields.

## Implementation notes
- Depends on canonical/hook usage detail fields.
- Do not invent cache numbers when absent.

## Out of scope
- Emitting cache_control on requests (separate issue)
- Billing dashboards

## Related
- Depends on: Usage details: cached_tokens, reasoning_tokens in hooks and OpenAI usage
- Anthropic cache_control passthrough on translate

title: image_url.detail mapping
body:
## Goal
Preserve OpenAI `image_url.detail` (`auto`|`low`|`high`) through canonical image blocks and map best-effort to other providers.

## Context
`imageURLObject` only has `URL`. `detail` is dropped on parse. Anthropic/Google do not have an identical field; fidelity is OpenAI round-trip plus documented best-effort or drop for others.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Canonical `ImageSource` (or Block) carries optional `Detail string`.
- [ ] OpenAI ingress parses `detail`; egress re-emits it on `image_url`.
- [ ] OpenAI → OpenAI-compat translate preserves detail.
- [ ] Anthropic/Google: document mapping or drop; golden test locks policy.
- [ ] Invalid detail values: accept pass-through string or validate against known set (choose one; test).

## Implementation notes
- Do not default detail to `auto` on the wire if client omitted it.

## Out of scope
- Changing image fetch/resize behavior based on detail
- Non-OpenAI detail enums invention

## Related
- Fix Google egress image URL
- Golden fixtures suite for translation drops

title: Google safetySettings passthrough on Google egress when from Google ingress (translate drop policy)
body:
## Goal
Define and implement policy for Gemini `safetySettings`: preserve when translating Google→Google is not passthrough, and document drops for non-Google clients.

## Context
True Google→Google same-family traffic uses passthrough (full fidelity). Translation rebuilds `generateRequest` without `safetySettings`, so any path that still translates Google requests (or maps OpenAI→Google) cannot express safety thresholds.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Policy written:
  - Google ingress → canonical optional `SafetySettings` raw or typed.
  - Google egress re-emits when present.
  - OpenAI/Anthropic ingress: no mapping unless a clear analogue exists; otherwise drop with golden test.
- [ ] Unit tests: Google request with safetySettings → canonical → Google body retains categories/thresholds.
- [ ] Confirm router still prefers passthrough for pure Google→Google; this issue covers translate-only gaps.
- [ ] README caveat documents safetySettings fidelity.

## Implementation notes
- Storing as `json.RawMessage` / typed slice is fine; avoid over-abstracting harm categories.
- Do not apply gateway-default safety settings unless configured explicitly (out of scope).

## Out of scope
- OpenAI content filter configuration mapping
- Anthropic safety/moderation APIs

## Related
- Google candidateCount / n multi-choice policy
- Golden fixtures suite for translation drops

title: Google candidateCount / n multi-choice policy (document drop or implement n=1 only clearly)
body:
## Goal
Make multi-candidate behavior explicit: either implement `n` / `candidateCount` properly or hard-document and test that translation only supports a single choice.

## Context
README lists OpenAI `n` as **dropped**. Canonical response has a single `Content` slice (one candidate). Google `candidateCount` is not modeled. Clients may assume multiple choices.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Choose policy:
  - **Recommended:** n>1 / candidateCount>1 on translate → `bad_request` with clear message, **or** coerce to 1 with documented drop + golden test.
  - Do not silently accept n=3 and return one choice without documentation.
- [ ] OpenAI ingress records `n` or rejects per policy.
- [ ] Google ingress/egress records `candidateCount` or rejects per policy.
- [ ] Serialize always returns exactly one choice/candidate under the n=1 policy.
- [ ] README translation table updated with explicit wording.
- [ ] Unit tests for n=1 success and n=2 policy behavior.

## Implementation notes
- Full multi-candidate support would require canonical response redesign (`[]Candidate`); that is a larger change — only pursue if product prioritizes it over explicit n=1.

## Out of scope
- logprobs / top_logprobs multi-choice coupling
- Streaming multi-choice

## Related
- Golden fixtures suite for translation drops
- README Translation feature table

title: Tool result multimodal content (not flatten to text only)
body:
## Goal
Allow tool results to carry multimodal content blocks (text, images, documents) instead of only a single string `Result`.

## Context
`canonical.Block` tool_result uses `Result string`. Anthropic tool_result `content` can be a string or array of blocks. OpenAI tool role content is often string but may be multipart in some stacks. Google `functionResponse` is JSON-centric. Current parsers flatten to string, losing images in tool results.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Canonical tool_result supports either:
  - `ResultBlocks []Block` in addition to string, or
  - non-text blocks nested under tool_result per agreed shape.
- [ ] Anthropic ingress/egress: array content preserved (text + image at minimum).
- [ ] OpenAI: string path still works; array content parsed when present; egress emits compatible shape.
- [ ] Google: best-effort JSON functionResponse or multipart per Gemini capabilities; document limits.
- [ ] Backward compatible: existing string-only fixtures still pass.
- [ ] Unit tests with image-in-tool_result Anthropic → OpenAI/Google.

## Implementation notes
- Avoid infinite nesting; tool_result should not contain tool_use.
- IsError flag remains.

## Out of scope
- Executing tools in the gateway
- Changing tool_use input schema handling

## Related
- Canonical BlockDocument
- parallel_tool_calls mapping

title: redacted_thinking blocks support
body:
## Goal
Support Anthropic `redacted_thinking` content blocks in canonical form so multi-turn Claude thinking sessions do not lose redacted segments.

## Context
`egress/anthropic/extra_test.go` currently expects `redacted_thinking` to be **skipped** (`TestParseBlockUnknown`). Dropping redacted thinking can break Anthropic’s required thinking history continuity for subsequent turns.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Canonical represents redacted thinking (new block type or `BlockThinking` flag `Redacted bool` + opaque data field).
- [ ] Anthropic parse/build/stream handle `redacted_thinking` (including any `data` payload).
- [ ] OpenAI mapping: opaque passthrough via extension field or omit with **documented** policy that is safe for Anthropic egress re-entry (prefer preserving opaque blob if history will return to Anthropic).
- [ ] Google mapping: documented best-effort (likely drop or opaque text) with tests.
- [ ] Unit tests replace “want skip” with “want preserve” for Anthropic path.
- [ ] Multi-turn fixture: thinking + redacted_thinking + text → Anthropic wire retains redacted block.

## Implementation notes
- Do not attempt to decrypt or display redacted payloads.
- Stream events may need a redacted-thinking start/delta variant or reuse thinking events with a flag.

## Out of scope
- Client UI for showing redacted placeholders beyond dialect norms
- Non-Anthropic redacted reasoning formats

## Related
- Round-trip reasoning_content on assistant history
- Streaming: preserve reasoning deltas across dialects

title: Non-function OpenAI tools policy (skip vs error)
body:
## Goal
Replace silent skipping of non-`function` OpenAI tools with an explicit, tested policy.

## Context
`ingress/openai/parse.go` `parseTools`:
```go
if t.Type != "" && t.Type != "function" {
    continue // only function tools are representable
}
```
Clients sending `type: custom` / built-in tools may believe tools were registered.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Product policy chosen and documented in README caveats:
  - **skip** (current) but log/metric + optional warning header, **or**
  - **error** `bad_request` listing unsupported tool types, **or**
  - **skip with structured warning** in response extensions (if any).
- [ ] Unit tests lock the policy for `type: function` (ok), empty type treated as function (current), and non-function types.
- [ ] Translation of function tools unchanged.
- [ ] If error policy: OpenAI error envelope remains correct.

## Implementation notes
- Prefer fail-fast error for unknown types if gateway aims for fidelity; prefer skip if aiming for maximum compatibility with mixed tool arrays.
- Record decision in golden drop list if skip.

## Out of scope
- Implementing OpenAI built-in tools execution
- Anthropic computer-use / special tool types beyond function declarations

## Related
- parallel_tool_calls mapping
- Golden fixtures suite for translation drops

title: max_completion_tokens vs max_tokens fidelity for reasoning models
body:
## Goal
Preserve the distinction between OpenAI `max_tokens` and `max_completion_tokens` where it matters for reasoning models, and map correctly on egress.

## Context
Ingress currently collapses both into `canonical.Request.MaxTokens`, preferring `max_tokens` then `max_completion_tokens`. OpenAI egress always emits `max_tokens` only (`BuildRequest` sets `MaxTokens`). For newer OpenAI reasoning models, `max_completion_tokens` is the supported knob and `max_tokens` may be rejected or mean something different. Cross-provider mapping still needs a single budget field but OpenAI egress should re-emit the appropriate field.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Canonical records enough information to rebuild the client’s preferred OpenAI field (e.g. source enum, or separate optional fields).
- [ ] OpenAI ingress: both fields parsed; if both set, documented precedence (match OpenAI or explicit error).
- [ ] OpenAI egress: emit `max_completion_tokens` when that was the ingress source or when targeting reasoning-model-safe wire; emit `max_tokens` when that was source / non-reasoning compat.
- [ ] Anthropic/Google egress continue to map the numeric budget to `max_tokens` / `maxOutputTokens`.
- [ ] Existing test `TestParseMaxCompletionTokens` updated/extended; new egress tests.
- [ ] Default Anthropic 4096 injection behavior unchanged when budget unset.

## Implementation notes
- Avoid sending both fields to OpenAI upstreams unless known-safe.
- openai_compat providers may only understand one field — prefer compatibility based on provider family config if available.

## Out of scope
- Automatic model family detection beyond existing config
- Separating reasoning budget from output budget as two independent canonical fields (unless needed by thinking config issue)

## Related
- Canonical reasoning / thinking config
- OpenAI reasoning_effort mapping

title: System fingerprint / service_tier optional passthrough
body:
## Goal
Optionally carry OpenAI `system_fingerprint` (response) and `service_tier` (request/response) through translation when present, without requiring them for other dialects.

## Context
These fields are used for debugging determinism and service tier selection. They are currently dropped on translate. Not all providers have analogues.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Request: OpenAI `service_tier` parsed into canonical optional field; OpenAI egress re-emits; other dialects omit.
- [ ] Response: OpenAI serialize can include `system_fingerprint` and `service_tier` when canonical response carries them from OpenAI egress parse.
- [ ] Anthropic/Google: no fake fingerprints invented.
- [ ] Unit tests for request round-trip OpenAI→openai_compat and response parse→serialize.
- [ ] Document as best-effort optional metadata in README.

## Implementation notes
- Store as simple strings on canonical Request/Response.
- Safe to omitempty everywhere.

## Out of scope
- Enforcing tier SLAs
- Mapping to non-OpenAI priority/latency hints unless trivial

## Related
- seed through canonical
- Usage details hooks

title: Golden fixtures suite for translation drops (explicit drop list tests)
body:
## Goal
Create an air-gapped golden fixture suite that locks **which fields are preserved vs explicitly dropped** on every dialect pair for chat translation.

## Context
Fidelity work will land incrementally. Without an explicit drop list, regressions reintroduce silent data loss (as with Google image placeholders and OpenAI thinking history). `testdata/fixtures/` exists but is empty; multimodal design calls for golden HTTP fixtures.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Fixture layout under `testdata/fixtures/chat_translate/` (or similar) with README explaining how fixtures are produced offline.
- [ ] For each ingress×egress pair among `{openai, anthropic, google}`:
  - at least one “kitchen sink” request JSON
  - expected egress wire JSON (or canonical snapshot)
  - expected **drop list** file listing fields intentionally removed
- [ ] Go tests load fixtures and fail when:
  - a dropped field reappears unexpectedly, or
  - a field marked preserved is missing/changed
- [ ] Covers known current drops at time of landing (e.g. `n`, `logprobs`, penalties/seed until implemented, `cache_control` if PT-only, non-function tools policy).
- [ ] Stream and non-stream variants for thinking deltas at least for one pair.
- [ ] CI runs fixtures with `go test` without network.

## Implementation notes
- Prefer comparing normalized JSON (stable key order) rather than raw bytes.
- Update drop lists in the same PR as fidelity features (definition of done for other M2 issues).

## Out of scope
- Live provider recordings in CI
- Media generation fixtures (other milestone)

## Related
- All Phase B mapping issues
- CONTRIBUTING.md translation vs passthrough guidance

title: DeepSeek/Kimi/Z.AI reasoning_content multi-turn tool-loop middleware (passthrough invariant)
body:
## Goal
Guarantee that OpenAI-compatible providers which require `reasoning_content` on subsequent assistant turns (DeepSeek, Kimi/Moonshot, Z.AI, etc.) keep that field intact on **passthrough** and on **translate-to-openai_compat** tool loops.

## Context
Several openai_compat models break multi-turn tool calling if prior assistant `reasoning_content` is stripped. Passthrough should already forward bytes, but model rewrite / stream_options injection must not re-serialize in a lossy way. Translate path must not drop thinking when egress is OpenAI-compatible (see related OpenAI egress issue).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Passthrough invariant test: request body with assistant `reasoning_content` + tool_calls + follow-up tool message is forwarded with reasoning field present after model rewrite.
- [ ] Translate path: Anthropic/Google thinking → OpenAI-compat egress includes `reasoning_content` on rebuilt history.
- [ ] Tool-loop fixture (≥2 iterations) asserting the field survives each hop through the gateway.
- [ ] Document provider quirk in README caveats (reasoning_content required for some openai_compat models).
- [ ] Stream and non-stream covered at least once.

## Implementation notes
- May be satisfied largely by fixing OpenAI egress thinking drop + ensuring passthrough does not JSON-recode messages.
- Add regression tests named after provider families even if using fake upstreams.

## Out of scope
- Vendor-specific non-OpenAI reasoning APIs
- Changing client SDKs

## Related
- Round-trip reasoning_content on assistant history (OpenAI egress must not drop thinking)
- Streaming: preserve reasoning deltas across dialects

title: Streaming: preserve reasoning deltas across dialects
body:
## Goal
Ensure reasoning/thinking stream deltas survive every ingress↔egress dialect combination without being coalesced away or mis-ordered relative to text/tool events.

## Context
Canonical already has `EventThinkingDelta` and partial support:
- Anthropic stream parse/serialize thinking blocks and `thinking_delta`
- OpenAI stream serialize maps thinking → `reasoning_content`
- Google stream maps thought parts
Gaps remain around OpenAI egress stream parse (ingesting upstream reasoning deltas into canonical), redacted thinking, signature timing, and Google thought signatures/ordering.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Matrix unit tests (fake SSE/JSON streams) for all 6 directed pairs among openai/anthropic/google:
  - thinking-only deltas
  - thinking then text
  - thinking then tool_use JSON deltas
- [ ] OpenAI upstream stream chunks with `reasoning_content` / delta reasoning parse into `EventThinkingDelta`.
- [ ] Anthropic client sees `content_block_start(type=thinking)` + `thinking_delta` when upstream was OpenAI/Google reasoning.
- [ ] OpenAI client sees `reasoning_content` deltas when upstream was Anthropic/Google.
- [ ] Google client sees `thought` parts / equivalent stream representation when upstream emits thinking.
- [ ] Final non-stream assemble path remains consistent with stream (no reasoning loss only on one path).
- [ ] Ordering invariant: tool call ids/names not interleaved incorrectly due to thinking blocks.
- [ ] Integration with redacted_thinking policy once implemented (skip or opaque events).

## Implementation notes
- Reuse existing stream serializer state machines; extend OpenAI egress stream parser symmetrically with ingress serializer.
- Avoid buffering entire thinking section unless a dialect requires atomic blocks (document if required).

## Out of scope
- Token-level identical chunk boundaries across providers
- UI progressive-render requirements beyond wire fidelity

## Related
- Round-trip reasoning_content on assistant history
- redacted_thinking blocks support
- Canonical reasoning / thinking config
- Golden fixtures suite for translation drops
